rationale: Keep: the citing paper’s core outcome is increased choice of “unique and self-expressive options” via private self-focus, and the cited 2011 paper directly theorizes and empirically tests self- vs other-expression as a common driver of unconventional consumer choices across multiple choice dilemmas. This strong construct-level overlap (self-expression/unconventional choice, uniqueness-related alternatives) suggests the citation is plausibly foundational/theoretical rather than an incidental mention, even though only DOI-level match evidence is available.
